displayTitle: The Indication Reports the Whole Process
kicker: PT LEVEL II // FACTORS AFFECTING INDICATIONS

beats.narration: Every indication reports two things at once — the flaw, and the process that revealed it. The same crack can blaze, glow faintly, or stay silent.

payload.title: READ THE PROCESS, NOT JUST THE FLAW
payload.sub: One crack, three brightnesses — the process moved, not the metal.

beats.narration: Four factor groups gate the result in sequence — flaw open, cavity clean, penetrant capable, technique disciplined. The weakest gate sets what you see.

payload.heading: Four gates, weakest one wins
payload.note: PRIOR PROCESSING · PRECLEANING · PENETRANT USED · TECHNIQUE

beats.narration: Each gate fails its own way. Prior processing smears flaws shut. Soil fills the cavity. The penetrant sets the ceiling. Technique loses it at the wash.

tiles.s: Soil or water blocks the cavity
Type and level fix the ceiling

beats.narration: Now read the pattern. One dim part points at that part — its cleaning or history. Every part dim, panel included, points at materials or equipment.

chips.s: Its own cleaning or prior history

beats.narration: So diagnose the factor before you judge the part. Never pass hardware on a process that could not have shown you the flaw.

payload.sub: Work the chain backward — one part, or every part?
